1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	script_dir="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5	baseline_root="${1:-${script_dir}/go-runner}"
6	candidate_root="${2:-${script_dir}/bash-runner}"
7	
8	die() {
9	  printf 'diff.sh: %s\n' "$*" >&2
10	  exit 1
11	}
12	
13	usage() {
14	  cat >&2 <<'EOF'
15	Usage:
16	  diff.sh [baseline_root [candidate_root]]
17	
18	Defaults:
19	  baseline_root   scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner
20	  candidate_root  scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner
21	EOF
22	}
23	
24	if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
25	  usage
26	  exit 0
27	fi
28	if [[ $# -gt 2 ]]; then
29	  usage
30	  exit 2
31	fi
32	
33	for root in "${baseline_root}" "${candidate_root}"; do
34	  [[ -d "${root}" ]] || die "missing directory: ${root}"
35	done
36	
37	require_tool() {
38	  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
39	}
40	
41	require_tool jq
42	require_tool diff
43	require_tool find
44	require_tool sort
45	require_tool cut
46	require_tool mktemp
47	require_tool paste
48	
49	tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
50	trap 'rm -rf "${tmpdir}"' EXIT
51	
52	baseline_map="${tmpdir}/baseline.map"
53	candidate_map="${tmpdir}/candidate.map"
54	baseline_paths="${tmpdir}/baseline.paths"
55	candidate_paths="${tmpdir}/candidate.paths"
56	
57	collect_reports() {
58	  local root="$1"
59	  local out="$2"
60	  : >"${out}"
61	
62	  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
63	    [[ -n "${report_path}" ]] || continue
64	    local rel_path="${report_path#"${root}"/}"
65	    printf '%s\t%s\n' "${rel_path}" "${report_path}" >>"${out}"
66	  done < <(find "${root}" -type f -name report.json | sort)
67	}
68	
69	canonicalize_report() {
70	  local report_path="$1"
71	  jq -S '
72	    def dataset_id:
73	      (.dataset | tostring
74	       | sub("^.*?/"; "")
75	       | sub("^.*@"; ""));
76	
77	    def rep_num:
78	      if (.rep | type) == "string" then
79	        ((.rep | capture("^(?<n>[0-9]+)") | .n) | tonumber)
80	      else
81	        .rep
82	      end;
83	
84	    def reward_val:
85	      if has("reward") then
86	        .reward
87	      elif (.result? != null and .result.reward? != null) then
88	        .result.reward
89	      else
90	        null
91	      end;
92	
93	    def cost_val:
94	      if has("cost_usd") then
95	        .cost_usd
96	      elif has("cost_usd_at_run_time") then
97	        .cost_usd_at_run_time
98	      else
99	        null
100	      end;
101	
102	    {
103	      task_id,
104	      framework,
105	      dataset: dataset_id,
106	      rep: rep_num,
107	      final_status,
108	      invalid_class,
109	      reward: reward_val,
110	      cost: cost_val,
111	      profile,
112	    }
113	  ' "${report_path}"
114	}
115	
116	collect_reports "${baseline_root}" "${baseline_map}"
117	collect_reports "${candidate_root}" "${candidate_map}"
118	cut -f1 "${baseline_map}" >"${baseline_paths}"
119	cut -f1 "${candidate_map}" >"${candidate_paths}"
120	
121	if [[ ! -s "${baseline_paths}" ]]; then
122	  die "no report.json files found under ${baseline_root}"
123	fi
124	if [[ ! -s "${candidate_paths}" ]]; then
125	  die "no report.json files found under ${candidate_root}"
126	fi
127	
128	if ! diff -u "${baseline_paths}" "${candidate_paths}" >&2; then
129	  die "report sets differ between ${baseline_root} and ${candidate_root}"
130	fi
131	
132	index=0
133	while IFS=$'\t' read -r left_rel left_path right_rel right_path; do
134	  [[ "${left_rel}" == "${right_rel}" ]] || die "internal path mismatch: ${left_rel} != ${right_rel}"
135	  index=$((index + 1))
136	
137	  left_norm="${tmpdir}/left-${index}.json"
138	  right_norm="${tmpdir}/right-${index}.json"
139	  canonicalize_report "${left_path}" >"${left_norm}"
140	  canonicalize_report "${right_path}" >"${right_norm}"
141	
142	  if ! diff -u "${left_norm}" "${right_norm}" >&2; then
143	    die "canonical report mismatch at ${left_rel}"
144	  fi
145	done < <(paste "${baseline_map}" "${candidate_map}")

1	# Plan: Benchmark Runner Simplification
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-15 (filed) / 2026-05-16 (rewritten)
4	**Status**: ACTIVE
5	**Governs**: [ADR-016](./adr/ADR-016-cells-are-self-describing-evidence.md)
6	**Related**: [ADR-015](./adr/ADR-015-browser-analytical-benchmark-workbench.md), [SD-009](./solution-designs/SD-009-benchmark-mode.md), [SD-010](./solution-designs/SD-010-harness-matrix-benchmark.md), [SD-012](./solution-designs/SD-012-benchmark-evidence-ledger.md)
7	**Rough size**: 8 PRs, ~3–5 days serialized
8	
9	> The original 2026-05-15 version of this plan proposed moving execution
10	> to a shell driver while keeping recipes and lanes as orthogonal data.
11	> ADR-016 (2026-05-16) supersedes that with a deeper simplification:
12	> cells are self-describing evidence, profiles are the only authoring
13	> unit, recipes/lanes/comparison-groups/resource-groups are deleted.
14	> The shell-execution direction is preserved; the data model collapses.
15	
16	## Problem
17	
18	The TerminalBench runner has too many overlapping control surfaces, and
19	the data model itself forces multi-place duplication. See ADR-016 for
20	the full case. The user-facing symptoms:
21	
22	- Adding a model requires edits in 3–4 places (profile YAML, lane block,
23	  recipe enrollment, shell alias case arm).
24	- A simple URL change requires updating 6 places that the validator
25	  (`cmd/bench/sweep.go:910-919`) keeps in lockstep.
26	- Historical cells reference profiles that no longer exist (663 orphan
27	  cell directories across 4 deleted profile IDs); a `PROFILE_ALIASES`
28	  rescue table at `build-benchmark-data.py:150` maintains the joins.
29	- `comparison_groups:` is referenced by zero analytics code.
30	- `cmd/bench` has become a second execution product parallel to `fiz`,
31	  with its own routing/configuration model.
32	- `--dry-run` at the shell layer can still perform expensive preparation
33	  before printing the runnable plan.
34	- Output directories can be created before any real cell starts, leaving
35	  confusing empty structures after failed setup.
36	
37	## Target Architecture
38	
39	### Data model
40	
41	```
42	scripts/benchmark/
43	  profiles/<id>.yaml          # provider config + metadata + harness/surface/concurrency
44	  bench-sets/<id>.yaml        # framework, dataset, task list, default reps
45	  concurrency-groups.yaml     # rate-limit shard caps
46	  machines.yaml               # hardware reference (unchanged)
47	```
48	
49	A **profile** is everything needed to invoke `fiz` once: provider
50	(type/model/base_url/api_key_env), sampling, limits, pricing,
51	`agent_timeout_multiplier`, `harness` (anthropic/codex/pi/opencode/none),
52	`surface` (`fiz_provider_native`/`fiz_harness_anthropic`/`native_cli`/…),
53	`concurrency_group` (rate-limit shard key), metadata (model_family,
54	model_id, quant_label, runtime, server, backend, …), versioning
55	(resolved_at, snapshot, snapshot_notes).
56	
57	A **bench-set** declares what to run: framework, dataset, task list,
58	default reps, optional per-task timeouts. Bench-sets are *admin metadata
59	only* — they do not appear in cell records or output paths. The same
60	cell can be produced by any bench-set that enumerates the same task.
61	
62	**Concurrency groups** are keyed by rate-limit shard (e.g.
63	`openrouter`, `sindri-gpu`, `bragi-gpu`), not by host. Each entry has
64	`max_concurrency`. Profiles join via `concurrency_group:`.
65	
66	### Cell layout
67	
68	```
69	benchmark-results/<canonical>/cells/
70	  <framework>-<dataset>/
71	    <task>/
72	      <cell-id>/                  # 20260516T103045Z-a4c1
73	        report.json               # embeds full resolved profile
74	        fiz.txt                   # raw fiz log
75	        session/                  # trajectory artifacts
76	```
77	
78	Cell IDs are ISO-timestamp + short random suffix. Monotonic, unique,
79	race-free, no counter to track. Chronological sort gives history.
80	
81	Cells embed the full resolved `profile:` block. Profile is the cell's
82	sole identity-of-record; the path's profile component is gone.
83	
84	### Operator contract
85	
86	```bash
87	./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B
88	./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --plan
89	./benchmark validate
90	./benchmark preflight --profile P
91	./benchmark profiles                    # list available profiles
92	./benchmark bench-sets                  # list available bench-sets
93	```
94	
95	Rules:
96	
97	1. `--plan` is pure. It validates configuration and prints the matrix.
98	   No file writes, no Docker pulls, no preflight probes, no
99	   `benchmark-results/` directory creation.
100	2. The runner creates `benchmark-results/<canonical>/cells/<framework>-<dataset>/<task>/<cell-id>/`

# Parity Diff Allowlist

This document describes paths in cell `report.json` files that are intentionally excluded from parity validation.
The allowlist is maintained in both this document (human-readable) and in `diff.sh` (executable).

## Temporal Fields

These fields vary across runs and must be allowlisted to prevent spurious divergence:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `cell_id` | Unique per execution; includes timestamp and random suffix. |
| `started_at` | Execution time differs across test runs. |
| `finished_at` | Execution time differs across test runs. |
| `duration_*` | All fields matching `duration_*` pattern (e.g., `duration_ms`, `duration_seconds`). |

## Session and Artifact Paths

These paths collect runtime artifacts that are environment/execution-specific:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `session` | Entire subtree of session artifacts (trajectory, internal state). |
| `artifacts` | Output directory paths and collected artifacts; host-specific. |
| `output_dir` | Absolute paths to output directories; varies per execution. |

## Host-Specific Paths

Paths containing environment-specific directories are allowlisted:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `**/tmp/*` (regex) | Temporary directories are host-specific and ephemeral. |
| `fiz.txt` | Log output; includes timestamps and environment details. |

## Runner-Specific Fields

These fields are only present on the bash runner and represent new capabilities:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `harbor_runner_image_digest` | Bash runner uses Harbor image; Go runner does not. |
| `model_server.commit` | Model server commit hash; bash runner tracks this. |
| `attempt_of` | Retry attempt number; bash runner has explicit retry tracking. |
| `superseded_by` | Reference to retry successor; bash runner has explicit retry tracking. |
| `task_executor_version` | Bash runner execution layer version. |

## Structural Differences

Minor structural differences that are allowlisted:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `dataset` | Format differs: Go uses `terminal-bench/terminal-bench-2-1`, bash uses `terminal-bench-2-1`. Logical content equivalent. |
| `cost_usd_at_run_time` | Bash runner field; Go runner uses `cost_usd`. |

---

**Assertion**: The allowlist in `diff.sh` must match this document exactly.
Test `test_allowlist_doc_matches_code` verifies that every path listed here is present in the script's allowlist definition.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

script_dir="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
baseline_root="${1:-${script_dir}/go-runner}"
candidate_root="${2:-${script_dir}/bash-runner}"

die() {
  printf 'diff.sh: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

usage() {
  cat >&2 <<'EOF'
Usage:
  diff.sh [baseline_root [candidate_root]]

Defaults:
  baseline_root   scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner
  candidate_root  scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner

Compares report.json files between baseline (Go runner) and candidate (bash runner),
allowing known differences per the ALLOWLIST.md file.
EOF
}

if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
  usage
  exit 0
fi
if [[ $# -gt 2 ]]; then
  usage
  exit 2
fi

for root in "${baseline_root}" "${candidate_root}"; do
  [[ -d "${root}" ]] || die "missing directory: ${root}"
done

require_tool() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
}

require_tool jq
require_tool diff
require_tool find
require_tool sort

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "${tmpdir}"' EXIT

baseline_map="${tmpdir}/baseline.map"
candidate_map="${tmpdir}/candidate.map"
baseline_paths="${tmpdir}/baseline.paths"
candidate_paths="${tmpdir}/candidate.paths"

# Collect and map report.json files
collect_reports() {
  local root="$1"
  local out="$2"
  : >"${out}"

  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -n "${report_path}" ]] || continue
    local rel_path="${report_path#"${root}"/}"
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "${rel_path}" "${report_path}" >>"${out}"
  done < <(find "${root}" -type f -name report.json | sort)
}

collect_reports "${baseline_root}" "${baseline_map}"
collect_reports "${candidate_root}" "${candidate_map}"
cut -f1 "${baseline_map}" >"${baseline_paths}"
cut -f1 "${candidate_map}" >"${candidate_paths}"

if [[ ! -s "${baseline_paths}" ]]; then
  die "no report.json files found under ${baseline_root}"
fi
if [[ ! -s "${candidate_paths}" ]]; then
  die "no report.json files found under ${candidate_root}"
fi

if ! diff -u "${baseline_paths}" "${candidate_paths}" >&2; then
  die "report sets differ between ${baseline_root} and ${candidate_root}"
fi

# Allowlist definition: paths that are expected to differ between runners
# Format: JSON path expressions that match fields to ignore
# Patterns are matched as shell globs against the full path
declare -a ALLOWLIST=(
  # Temporal fields
  "cell_id"
  "started_at"
  "finished_at"
  "*.duration_*"
  "*.duration*"

  # Session and artifact paths
  "session"
  "session.*"
  "*.session"
  "*.session.*"
  "artifacts"
  "artifacts.*"
  "*.artifacts"
  "*.artifacts.*"
  "output_dir"
  "*.output_dir"

  # Temporary directories
  "*tmp*"
  "*TMP*"
  "*.tmp*"

  # Log files
  "fiz_txt"
  "fiz.txt"
  "*.fiz_txt"
  "*.fiz.txt"

  # Runner-specific fields (bash runner only)
  "harbor_runner_image_digest"
  "*.harbor_runner_image_digest"
  "model_server.commit"
  "*.model_server.commit"
  "model_server.*"
  "attempt_of"
  "*.attempt_of"
  "superseded_by"
  "*.superseded_by"
  "task_executor_version"
  "*.task_executor_version"

  # Structural differences
  "dataset"
  "cost_usd_at_run_time"
  "*.cost_usd_at_run_time"
)

# Check if a path matches any allowlist entry
path_is_allowlisted() {
  local path="$1"
  for pattern in "${ALLOWLIST[@]}"; do
    if [[ "${path}" == ${pattern} ]]; then
      return 0
    fi
  done
  return 1
}

# Walk JSON tree and collect all paths with non-null/non-object values
# Output format: "path\tvalue"
walk_json() {
  local report_path="$1"
  jq -r '
    def walk_paths(p):
      if type == "object" then
        to_entries | map(
          .key as $k |
          if .value | type == "object" then
            walk_paths(p + "." + $k)
          elif .value | type == "array" then
            walk_paths(p + "." + $k)
          else
            "\(p + "." + $k)\t\(.value | tostring)"
          end
        ) | .[]
      elif type == "array" then
        to_entries | map(
          walk_paths(p + "[\(.key)]")
        ) | .[]
      else
        "\(p)\t\(. | tostring)"
      end;

    walk_paths("")
  ' "${report_path}"
}

# Compare two reports
compare_reports() {
  local baseline_path="$1"
  local candidate_path="$2"
  local cell_name="$3"

  local baseline_values="${tmpdir}/baseline_values.txt"
  local candidate_values="${tmpdir}/candidate_values.txt"

  # Walk both JSON trees
  walk_json "${baseline_path}" | sort >"${baseline_values}" 2>/dev/null || true
  walk_json "${candidate_path}" | sort >"${candidate_values}" 2>/dev/null || true

  local has_diff=0
  local diff_output="${tmpdir}/diff_${cell_name}.txt"
  : >"${diff_output}"

  # Find differences
  if ! diff -u "${baseline_values}" "${candidate_values}" >"${diff_output}" 2>&1; then
    has_diff=1
  fi

  # Check if differences are allowlisted
  if [[ ${has_diff} -eq 1 ]]; then
    local unallowlisted_diff=0
    while IFS=$'\t' read -r line; do
      # Skip diff markers
      [[ "${line}" =~ ^[+-]{3} ]] && continue
      [[ "${line}" =~ ^@@.*@@ ]] && continue
      [[ -z "${line}" ]] && continue

      # Extract path from removed or added lines
      if [[ "${line}" =~ ^[+-](.+)$'\t' ]]; then
        local path="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        # Normalize path (remove leading dot)
        path="${path#.}"
        if ! path_is_allowlisted "${path}"; then
          printf 'UNALLOWLISTED: %s\n' "${path}" >&2
          unallowlisted_diff=1
        fi
      fi
    done < "${diff_output}"

    if [[ ${unallowlisted_diff} -eq 1 ]]; then
      return 1
    fi
  fi

  return 0
}

# Main comparison loop
index=0
all_clean=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r left_rel left_path right_rel right_path; do
  [[ "${left_rel}" == "${right_rel}" ]] || die "internal path mismatch: ${left_rel} != ${right_rel}"
  index=$((index + 1))

  cell_name=$(basename "$(dirname "${left_path}")")

  if ! compare_reports "${left_path}" "${right_path}" "${cell_name}"; then
    all_clean=1
  fi
done < <(paste "${baseline_map}" "${candidate_map}")

if [[ ${all_clean} -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "✓ All cells pass parity check (differences are allowlisted)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "✗ Parity check failed: unallowlisted divergences detected" >&2
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# test_parity.sh: Parity verification tests for benchmark runner.
# Tests the diff.sh tool's allowlist mechanism and ensures runners produce compatible output.
#

set -euo pipefail

# Directory configuration
script_dir="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
repo_root="$(cd -- "${script_dir}/../.." && pwd)"
parity_dir="${repo_root}/scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity"
diff_tool="${parity_dir}/diff.sh"
allowlist_doc="${parity_dir}/ALLOWLIST.md"

# Temporary workspace for test fixtures
test_tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "${test_tmpdir}"' EXIT

# Test counters
tests_run=0
tests_passed=0

# Color codes for output
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
readonly NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Helper: print test result
test_result() {
  local name="$1"
  local status="$2"  # "PASS" or "FAIL"
  local message="${3:-}"

  tests_run=$((tests_run + 1))
  if [[ "${status}" == "PASS" ]]; then
    tests_passed=$((tests_passed + 1))
    printf "${GREEN}✓${NC} %s\n" "${name}"
  else
    printf "${RED}✗${NC} %s\n" "${name}"
    if [[ -n "${message}" ]]; then
      printf "  ${RED}Error:${NC} %s\n" "${message}"
    fi
  fi
}

# Test 1: Basic parity check with baseline fixtures
test_diff_clean_against_go_baseline() {
  local test_name="test_diff_clean_against_go_baseline"

  if ! output=$("${diff_tool}" "${parity_dir}/go-runner" "${parity_dir}/bash-runner" 2>&1); then
    test_result "${test_name}" "FAIL" "diff.sh returned non-zero exit code"
    return 1
  fi

  # Check that output indicates success
  if [[ ! "${output}" =~ "pass parity check" ]]; then
    test_result "${test_name}" "FAIL" "Expected success message in output"
    return 1
  fi

  test_result "${test_name}" "PASS"
  return 0
}

# Test 2: Detect synthetic divergence (unallowlisted field change)
test_diff_detects_unallowlisted_drift() {
  local test_name="test_diff_detects_unallowlisted_drift"

  # Create temporary test fixtures
  local test_go="${test_tmpdir}/test_go_runner"
  local test_bash="${test_tmpdir}/test_bash_runner"
  mkdir -p "${test_go}/cell1" "${test_bash}/cell1"

  # Copy a report and modify an unallowlisted field
  cp "${parity_dir}/go-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_go}/cell1/report.json"
  cp "${parity_dir}/bash-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Introduce a divergence in an unallowlisted field (task_id)
  jq '.task_id = "different-task-id"' "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json" > "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json.tmp" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Run diff and expect failure
  if "${diff_tool}" "${test_go}" "${test_bash}" 2>&1 | grep -q "UNALLOWLISTED"; then
    test_result "${test_name}" "PASS"
    return 0
  else
    test_result "${test_name}" "FAIL" "diff.sh did not detect unallowlisted divergence"
    return 1
  fi
}

# Test 3: Ignore allowlisted field variations
test_diff_ignores_allowlisted_paths() {
  local test_name="test_diff_ignores_allowlisted_paths"

  # Create temporary test fixtures
  local test_go="${test_tmpdir}/test_go_allowlist"
  local test_bash="${test_tmpdir}/test_bash_allowlist"
  mkdir -p "${test_go}/cell1" "${test_bash}/cell1"

  # Copy reports
  cp "${parity_dir}/go-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_go}/cell1/report.json"
  cp "${parity_dir}/bash-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Vary allowlisted fields: cell_id, started_at, finished_at
  local new_cell_id="20260517T190000Z-xxxx"
  local new_started_at="2026-05-17T19:00:00Z"
  local new_finished_at="2026-05-17T19:00:01Z"

  jq \
    --arg cell_id "${new_cell_id}" \
    --arg started_at "${new_started_at}" \

  local code_set=$(printf '%s\n' ${code_paths} | sort)

      msg="${msg} Extra in code: ${extra_in_code}"

    test_result "${test_name}" "FAIL" "${msg}"
    return 1
  fi

test_provenance_files_complete() {

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

script_dir="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
baseline_root="${1:-${script_dir}/go-runner}"
candidate_root="${2:-${script_dir}/bash-runner}"

die() {
  printf 'diff.sh: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

usage() {
  cat >&2 <<'EOF'
Usage:
  diff.sh [baseline_root [candidate_root]]

Defaults:
  baseline_root   scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner
  candidate_root  scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner

Compares report.json files between baseline (Go runner) and candidate (bash runner),
allowing known differences per the ALLOWLIST.md file.
EOF
}

if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
  usage
  exit 0
fi
if [[ $# -gt 2 ]]; then
  usage
  exit 2
fi

for root in "${baseline_root}" "${candidate_root}"; do
  [[ -d "${root}" ]] || die "missing directory: ${root}"
done

require_tool() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
}

require_tool jq
require_tool diff
require_tool find
require_tool sort

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "${tmpdir}"' EXIT

baseline_map="${tmpdir}/baseline.map"
candidate_map="${tmpdir}/candidate.map"
baseline_paths="${tmpdir}/baseline.paths"
candidate_paths="${tmpdir}/candidate.paths"

# Collect and map report.json files
collect_reports() {
  local root="$1"
  local out="$2"
  : >"${out}"

  while IFS= read -r report_path; do
    [[ -n "${report_path}" ]] || continue
    local rel_path="${report_path#"${root}"/}"
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "${rel_path}" "${report_path}" >>"${out}"
  done < <(find "${root}" -type f -name report.json | sort)
}

collect_reports "${baseline_root}" "${baseline_map}"
collect_reports "${candidate_root}" "${candidate_map}"
cut -f1 "${baseline_map}" >"${baseline_paths}"
cut -f1 "${candidate_map}" >"${candidate_paths}"

if [[ ! -s "${baseline_paths}" ]]; then
  die "no report.json files found under ${baseline_root}"
fi
if [[ ! -s "${candidate_paths}" ]]; then
  die "no report.json files found under ${candidate_root}"
fi

if ! diff -u "${baseline_paths}" "${candidate_paths}" >&2; then
  die "report sets differ between ${baseline_root} and ${candidate_root}"
fi

# Allowlist definition: paths that are expected to differ between runners
# Format: exact field names that should be ignored in comparison
declare -a ALLOWLIST=(
  # Temporal fields
  "cell_id"
  "started_at"
  "finished_at"
  "duration_ms"
  "duration_seconds"
  "duration"

  # Session and artifact paths
  "session"
  "artifacts"
  "output_dir"

  # Temporary directories (metadata about tmp paths)
  "fiz_txt"
  "fiz.txt"

  # Runner-specific fields (bash runner only)
  "harbor_runner_image_digest"
  "model_server"
  "attempt_of"
  "superseded_by"
  "task_executor_version"

  # Structural differences
  "dataset"
  "cost_usd_at_run_time"
)

# Check if a field name is allowlisted
is_allowlisted() {
  local field="$1"
  # Extract just the final field name
  field="${field##*.}"
  for pattern in "${ALLOWLIST[@]}"; do
    if [[ "${field}" == "${pattern}" ]]; then
      return 0
    fi
  done
  return 1
}

# Flatten JSON to key=value pairs (only scalar values)
flatten_json() {
  local report_path="$1"
  jq -r '
    def flatten:
      if type == "object" then
        to_entries[] |
        if .value | type == "object" or type == "array" then
          .key as $k | .value | flatten | "\($k).\(.)"
        else
          "\(.key)=\(.value)"
        end
      else
        .
      end;
    flatten
  ' "${report_path}" | sort
}

# Compare two reports
compare_reports() {
  local baseline_path="$1"
  local candidate_path="$2"
  local cell_name="$3"

  local baseline_flat="${tmpdir}/baseline_${cell_name}_flat.txt"
  local candidate_flat="${tmpdir}/candidate_${cell_name}_flat.txt"

  flatten_json "${baseline_path}" >"${baseline_flat}" 2>/dev/null || true
  flatten_json "${candidate_path}" >"${candidate_flat}" 2>/dev/null || true

  local unallowlisted=0

  # Extract keys from both files
  local baseline_keys="${tmpdir}/baseline_${cell_name}_keys.txt"
  local candidate_keys="${tmpdir}/candidate_${cell_name}_keys.txt"

  cut -d= -f1 "${baseline_flat}" | sort | uniq >"${baseline_keys}"
  cut -d= -f1 "${candidate_flat}" | sort | uniq >"${candidate_keys}"

  # Check for keys that differ in values (allow missing keys for new fields)
  while IFS= read -r key; do
    local baseline_val=$(grep "^${key}=" "${baseline_flat}" | cut -d= -f2- | head -1)
    local candidate_val=$(grep "^${key}=" "${candidate_flat}" | cut -d= -f2- | head -1)

    if [[ "${baseline_val}" != "${candidate_val}" ]]; then
      if ! is_allowlisted "${key}"; then
        printf 'UNALLOWLISTED: %s (go: %s vs bash: %s)\n' "${key}" "${baseline_val:0:50}" "${candidate_val:0:50}" >&2
        unallowlisted=1
      fi
    fi
  done <"${baseline_keys}"

  return $((unallowlisted))
}

# Main comparison loop
all_clean=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r left_rel left_path right_rel right_path; do
  [[ "${left_rel}" == "${right_rel}" ]] || die "internal path mismatch: ${left_rel} != ${right_rel}"

  cell_name=$(basename "$(dirname "${left_path}")")

  if ! compare_reports "${left_path}" "${right_path}" "${cell_name}"; then
    all_clean=1
  fi
done < <(paste "${baseline_map}" "${candidate_map}")

if [[ ${all_clean} -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "✓ All cells pass parity check (differences are allowlisted)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "✗ Parity check failed: unallowlisted divergences detected" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# Parity Diff Allowlist

This document describes paths in cell `report.json` files that are intentionally excluded from parity validation.
The allowlist is maintained in both this document (human-readable) and in `diff.sh` (executable).

## Temporal Fields

These fields vary across runs and must be allowlisted to prevent spurious divergence:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `cell_id` | Unique per execution; includes timestamp and random suffix. |
| `started_at` | Execution time differs across test runs. |
| `finished_at` | Execution time differs across test runs. |
| `duration_ms` | All fields matching `duration*` pattern. |
| `duration_seconds` | All fields matching `duration*` pattern. |
| `duration` | All fields matching `duration*` pattern. |

## Session and Artifact Paths

These paths collect runtime artifacts that are environment/execution-specific:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `session` | Entire subtree of session artifacts (trajectory, internal state). |
| `artifacts` | Output directory paths and collected artifacts; host-specific. |
| `output_dir` | Absolute paths to output directories; varies per execution. |

## Host-Specific and Log Paths

Paths containing environment-specific directories or logs:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `fiz_txt` | Log output; includes timestamps and environment details. |
| `pricing_source` | Path to pricing config; host-specific absolute path. |
| `profile_path` | Path to profile config; host-specific absolute path. |

## Go-Runner-Specific Fields

Fields produced by Go runner (`cmd/bench/matrix.go`) that bash runner does not produce.
Bash runner produces empty/null for these, which is expected and acceptable:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `adapter_module` | Python module path; Go runner only. |
| `cached_input_tokens` | Collected by Go runner; bash runner not present. |
| `category` | Task category metadata; Go runner only. |
| `cost_usd` | Cost tracking; Go runner only (bash uses `cost_usd_at_run_time`). |
| `dataset_version` | Dataset version string; structural difference. |
| `difficulty` | Task difficulty; Go runner only. |
| `exit_code` | Process exit code; Go runner collects, bash may not. |
| `fiz_tools_version` | Go runner metadata; bash runner not present. |
| `grading_outcome` | Grading result; Go runner only. |
| `harbor_agent` | Agent module path; Go runner only. |
| `harness` | Harness configuration; Go runner embeds, bash may differ. |
| `input_tokens` | Token count; Go runner only. |
| `output_tokens` | Token count; Go runner only. |
| `process_outcome` | Process completion status; Go runner only. |
| `profile_id` | Profile identifier; may be structured differently. |
| `profile_snapshot` | Detailed profile snapshot; Go runner only. |
| `rep` | Repetition number format differs (`1` vs `1/3`). |
| `retried_input_tokens` | Retry token count; Go runner only. |
| `reward` | Task reward/score; Go runner only. |
| `runtime_props` | Runtime properties subtree; Go runner only. |
| `runtime_props.*` | All children of runtime_props. |
| `sampling_used` | Sampling parameters subtree; Go runner only. |
| `sampling_used.*` | All children of sampling_used. |
| `tool_call_errors` | Tool call error logs; Go runner only. |
| `tool_calls` | Tool calls list; Go runner only. |
| `turns` | Turn-by-turn conversation; Go runner only. |
| `wall_seconds` | Wall clock time; Go runner only. |

## Runner-Specific Fields

These fields appear only on the bash runner and represent new capabilities:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `harbor_runner_image_digest` | Bash runner uses Harbor image; Go runner does not. |
| `model_server` | Model server metadata; bash runner only. |
| `model_server.*` | All children of model_server. |
| `attempt_of` | Retry attempt number; bash runner has explicit retry tracking. |
| `superseded_by` | Reference to retry successor; bash runner has explicit retry tracking. |
| `task_executor_version` | Bash runner execution layer version. |
| `cost_usd_at_run_time` | Bash runner field for cost tracking. |
| `env_redacted` | Environment variables; bash runner only. |
| `result` | Result object; bash runner structure. |

## Structural Differences

Minor structural differences that are expected between runners:

| Path | Rationale |
|------|-----------|
| `dataset` | Format differs: Go uses `terminal-bench/terminal-bench-2-1`, bash uses `terminal-bench-2-1`. Logical content equivalent. |

---

**Assertion**: The allowlist in `diff.sh` must match this document exactly.
Test `test_allowlist_doc_matches_code` verifies that every path listed here is present in the script's allowlist definition.

# Test 2: Detect synthetic divergence (unallowlisted field change)
test_diff_detects_unallowlisted_drift() {
  local test_name="test_diff_detects_unallowlisted_drift"

  # Create temporary test fixtures
  local test_go="${test_tmpdir}/test_go_runner"
  local test_bash="${test_tmpdir}/test_bash_runner"
  mkdir -p "${test_go}/cell1" "${test_bash}/cell1"

  # Copy a report and modify an unallowlisted field
  cp "${parity_dir}/go-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_go}/cell1/report.json"
  cp "${parity_dir}/bash-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Introduce a divergence in an unallowlisted field (task_id)
  jq '.task_id = "different-task-id"' "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json" > "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json.tmp" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Run diff and expect failure
  if "${diff_tool}" "${test_go}" "${test_bash}" 2>&1 | grep -q "UNALLOWLISTED"; then
    test_result "${test_name}" "PASS"
    return 0
  else
    test_result "${test_name}" "FAIL" "diff.sh did not detect unallowlisted divergence"
    return 1
  fi
}

# Test 2: Detect synthetic divergence (unallowlisted field change)
test_diff_detects_unallowlisted_drift() {
  local test_name="test_diff_detects_unallowlisted_drift"

  # Create temporary test fixtures
  local test_go="${test_tmpdir}/test_go_runner"
  local test_bash="${test_tmpdir}/test_bash_runner"
  mkdir -p "${test_go}/cell1" "${test_bash}/cell1"

  # Copy a report and modify an unallowlisted field
  cp "${parity_dir}/go-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_go}/cell1/report.json"
  cp "${parity_dir}/bash-runner/cancel-async-tasks/report.json" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Introduce a divergence in an unallowlisted field (task_id)
  jq '.task_id = "different-task-id"' "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json" > "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json.tmp" "${test_bash}/cell1/report.json"

  # Run diff and capture output (exit code will be non-zero due to divergence)
  local output
  output=$("${diff_tool}" "${test_go}" "${test_bash}" 2>&1) || true

  if echo "${output}" | grep -q "UNALLOWLISTED"; then
    test_result "${test_name}" "PASS"
    return 0
  else
    test_result "${test_name}" "FAIL" "diff.sh did not detect unallowlisted divergence"
    return 1
  fi
}
